summary: Jeff Atwood enthusiastically endorses the use of programming cheatsheets as quick reference tools for developers. He traces his appreciation back to classic Beagle Brothers style references and highlights modern equivalents like VisiBone's regex reference. The post serves primarily as a curated collection of freely available cheatsheets covering web technologies, databases, and Microsoft .NET development. Atwood playfully suggests that displaying these references creates an impression of technical expertise while genuinely valuing their practical utility.
key_points: Densely packed reference charts serve both practical purposes and signal technical competence
VisiBone's JavaScript regex reference is highlighted as the best concise regex resource available
A comprehensive collection of free cheatsheets exists for web technologies including CSS, PHP, MySQL, and JavaScript
Microsoft .NET developers have access to keyboard references and formatting guides for Visual Studio languages
Cheatsheets come in various formats (PDF, PNG, wall charts, foldouts) to suit different usage preferences
money_quotes: Nothing promotes the illusion of mastery like a densely packed chart of obscure reference information.
Just throw some of those babies up on your walls and people will know that they're clearly dealing with a coding genius!
The Visibone regular expression reference is the best concise regex reference I've found to date.
key_insight: Programming cheatsheets are valuable quick-reference tools that both aid productivity and project an image of technical mastery.
